title: ferray-core: no datetime/timedelta dtype support
description:
Goal: 

Acceptance criteria:
- 

NumPy has datetime64 and timedelta64 dtypes with configurable time units (ns, us, ms, s, m, h, D, etc.), backed by int64 storage. These are essential for time series data. ferray-core has no datetime support at all. At minimum, a DType::DateTime64 and DType::Timedelta64 with a TimeUnit enum would allow round-tripping .npy files containing time data. The chrono crate could back the Rust-side representation.
